# `module_policy`

The `module_policy` block defines policies for your workspaces' Terraform modules. Module policies allow you to set guardrails at the abstraction layer where infrastructure is often packaged and shared. The first label of the block specifies the module source. The second label is a unique name for the policy itself. You can use a wildcard (*) for the module source to apply the policy to every module in the workspace.

@include "beta.mdx"

## Configuration model

The `module_policy` block supports the following configuration:

- `module_policy` &nbsp "[`<module_source>`](#module-source)" &nbsp "[`<policy_name>`](#policy-name)" &nbsp block
  - [`enforcement_level`](#enforcement_level) &nbsp string | optional
  - [`filter`](#filter) &nbsp boolean expression | optional
  - [`locals`](#locals) &nbsp block | optional
    - `<name>` &nbsp any
  - [`enforce`](#enforce) &nbsp block | required
    - [`condition`](#condition) &nbsp boolean expression | required
    - [`error_message`](#error_message) &nbsp string | optional
    - [`info_message`](#info_message) &nbsp string | optional

## Complete configuration example

The following example demonstrates a module policy block that matches all modules with all configuration options specified, and a module policy block that matches a specified module:

<CodeBlockConfig filename="policies/example.policy.hcl">

```hcl
module_policy "*" "version_and_source_check" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory"

  enforce {
    condition     = core::contains(local.approved_sources, meta.source)
    error_message = "The module source ${meta.source} is not in the approved list."
  }
  
  enforce {
    condition     = core::semverconstraint(meta.version, ">= 1.0.0")
    error_message = "Module version must be at least 1.0.0."
  }
}

module_policy "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc" "specific_version_check" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory"

  enforce {
    condition     = core::semverconstraint(meta.version, ">= 2.0.0")
    error_message = "The official VPC module must be at version 2.0.0 or higher."
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

## Specification

### Labels

| Label | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| First label | Yes | The module source (For example: `"./modules/networking"`, `"git::github.com/org/module"`). Use `*` to match all modules. |
| Second label | Yes | A unique name for this policy within the policy file. |

### Arguments

Module policy blocks support the following arguments.

| Argument | Required | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `enforcement_level` | No | String | `"mandatory"` | The enforcement level for the policy. Valid values: `"advisory"`, `"mandatory"`, `"mandatory_overridable"`. |
| `filter` | No | Boolean expression | - | Filter which modules the policy applies to based on their attributes. |

### Attributes

Within the `module_policy {}` block, you have access to the following attributes.

| Object | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `attrs` | Provides access to the module input variables. Note: Module policies do not support regular attributes at this time. |
| `meta` | Provides access to Terraform-defined information about the module, including `source`, `version`, and `address`. |

Each Terraform module defines the attributes available. Refer to the module documentation for a list of supported attributes.

#### `meta` object attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `meta.source` | String | The source of the module (For example: `"./modules/networking"`, `"git::github.com/org/module"`). |
| `meta.version` | String | The version of the module, if specified. |
| `meta.address` | String | The logical address of the module within the configuration. |

### The `enforce {}` block

Each `module_policy {}` block must contain one or more `enforce {}` blocks that define conditions that must evaluate to `true` for the policy to pass.

#### Arguments

| Argument | Required | Type | Description |
|----------|----------|------|-------------|
| `condition` | Yes | Boolean expression | A boolean expression that must evaluate to `true` for the policy to pass. |
| `error_message` | No | String | The error message to display when the condition evaluates to `false`. |
| `info_message` | No | String | An informational message to display regardless of whether the policy passes or fails. |

You can include one or more `enforce {}` blocks in each policy block. If any of the enforce blocks' conditions fail during evaluation, then Terraform policy will fail the policy and return the corresponding error messages.

## Examples

### Enforce approved module sources

```hcl
locals {
  approved_sources = [
    "./modules/networking",
    "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc",
    "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-security"
  ]
}

module_policy "*" "approved_sources_only" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory"

  enforce {
    condition     = core::contains(local.approved_sources, meta.source)
    error_message = "Module source ${meta.source} is not in the approved list. Only approved modules may be used."
  }
}
```

### Enforce minimum module version

```hcl
module_policy "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc" "minimum_version" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory"

  enforce {
    condition     = core::semverconstraint(meta.version, ">= 2.0.0")
    error_message = "The VPC module must be at version 2.0.0 or higher."
  }
}
```

### Enforce module version constraints

```hcl
module_policy "*" "version_requirements" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory_overridable"

  enforce {
    condition     = meta.version != null
    error_message = "All modules must specify a version."
  }

  enforce {
    condition     = core::semverconstraint(meta.version, ">= 1.0.0")
    error_message = "Module version must be at least 1.0.0."
  }
}
```

### Combine source and version checks

```hcl
locals {
  approved_sources = [
    "./modules/networking",
    "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc"
  ]
}

module_policy "*" "comprehensive_check" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory"

  enforce {
    condition     = core::contains(local.approved_sources, meta.source)
    error_message = "The module source ${meta.source} is not in the approved list."
  }
  
  enforce {
    condition     = core::semverconstraint(meta.version, ">= 1.0.0")
    error_message = "Module version must be at least 1.0.0."
    info_message  = "Current module version: ${meta.version}"
  }
}
```

### Specific module with detailed checks

```hcl
module_policy "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc" "vpc_module_policy" {
  enforcement_level = "mandatory"

  enforce {
    condition     = core::semverconstraint(meta.version, ">= 2.0.0, < 3.0.0")
    error_message = "The VPC module must be version 2.x.x."
  }

  enforce {
    condition     = meta.source == "git::github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc"
    error_message = "Only the official organization VPC module is permitted."
  }
}
```
